skill.name: tanstack-query
skill.description: Guides React developers through TanStack Query v5 setup, data fetching, mutations, caching, DevTools, testing, and v4 to v5 migration.
skill.summary: Build and migrate React server-state workflows with TanStack Query v5.
skill.author: jezweb

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical combined-risk heuristic, but the reviewed evidence is documentation and template code for TanStack Query usage. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or real command execution was found; remaining concerns are normal network examples and a placeholder shell script.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The many external command and weak-crypto detections are from Markdown code fences, migration tables, and TypeScript examples. Reviewed locations show educational TanStack Query snippets, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation or fenced TypeScript examples. No shell interpolation, runtime execution path, or cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Template Network Calls Are Expected Examples
low_findings.description: The templates use fetch calls to JSONPlaceholder and local API paths to demonstrate queries and mutations. This is legitimate for a data-fetching skill, but users should replace sample endpoints before production use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are clear tutorial endpoints and normal API examples. They do not include secrets or hidden destinations, but copied templates can perform outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Script Has No Harmful Logic
low_findings.description: The shell script is a placeholder with set -e and an echo statement. It is safe as shipped, but marketplaces should still label the skill as containing a script file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The complete script contains only comments, set -e, and a static echo. There is no command injection vector, file modification, network access, or credential access.

low_findings.title: DevTools Sensitive-Access Detections Are Benign
low_findings.description: The environment and key-file alerts map to import.meta.env.DEV and KeyboardEvent key handling in a DevTools example. These are normal frontend checks, not environment file reads or certificate access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines use a development-mode boolean and keyboard shortcut handling. The same file warns not to expose production DevTools or sensitive cache data.

content.user_title: Build TanStack Query v5 React Data Flows
content.value_statement: React apps often duplicate server-state logic and break during TanStack Query upgrades. This skill provides v5 patterns, migration guidance, and templates for queries, mutations, caching, DevTools, and tests.
actual_capabilities: Configures QueryClient defaults for stale time, garbage collection, retries, and refetch behavior.
Provides v5 useQuery, useMutation, and useInfiniteQuery patterns with TypeScript examples.
Explains optimistic updates, invalidation, rollback, and cross-component mutation tracking.
Guides v4 to v5 migration for object syntax, gcTime, isPending, and placeholderData changes.
Includes DevTools, error boundary, testing, and reusable custom hook templates.
limitations: Examples use sample endpoints and must be adapted to each application API.
It does not inspect a live project unless the AI tool is given project files.
It focuses on React integration, not every TanStack Query framework adapter.
Generated code still needs application-specific security and data validation review.

use_cases.title: Add Query Foundations
use_cases.description: Set up QueryClient, providers, query hooks, mutation hooks, and cache defaults in a React app.

use_cases.title: Migrate React Query v4 Code
use_cases.description: Update old React Query patterns to TanStack Query v5 object syntax, status names, and cache options.

use_cases.title: Debug Server-State Behavior
use_cases.description: Diagnose refetch loops, hydration issues, stale data, optimistic updates, and error boundary behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Query Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new React app
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up TanStack Query v5 in my React app. Include the provider, QueryClient defaults, one typed query hook, and loading and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Add Mutations and Invalidation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create TanStack Query v5 mutation hooks for creating, updating, and deleting records. Add cache invalidation and clear TypeScript input types.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate v4 Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React Query v4 code and convert it to TanStack Query v5. Fix object syntax, gcTime, isPending, and placeholderData changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design Advanced Server State
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a TanStack Query v5 architecture for this app. Include query key factories, optimistic updates, infinite queries, error boundaries, DevTools, and testing strategy.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for a typed todo query hook.
output_examples.output: The assistant describes a reusable query options factory, a typed fetch function, a custom hook, and component states for loading, error, fetching, and success.

output_examples.input: A team is migrating a paginated v4 query.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies removed v4 options, recommends placeholder data, adds explicit initial page parameters, and explains the user-visible behavior change.

output_examples.input: A production app keeps refetching on focus.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks stale time, refetch-on-window-focus, mount behavior, query keys, and DevTools observations before suggesting configuration changes.

best_practices: Use stable query keys and shared query option factories for reusable data access.
Set stale time, retry, and garbage collection defaults based on actual product behavior.
Keep sample endpoints, DevTools, and cache contents reviewed before production release.
anti_patterns: Do not copy v4 positional query syntax into v5 code.
Do not store secrets or unnecessary personal data in the client-side query cache.
Do not invalidate broad query groups when a narrow key update is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill support TanStack Query v5?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation and templates focus on TanStack Query v5 for React.

faq.question: Can it migrate React Query v4 code?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers common v4 to v5 changes, including object syntax, gcTime, isPending, and placeholderData.

faq.question: Does it include TypeScript examples?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates use TypeScript types for query data, mutation inputs, and custom hooks.

faq.question: Are the API URLs production ready?
faq.answer: No. The templates use sample endpoints and local API paths that users must replace.

faq.question: Does it cover testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes testing guidance using mocked server responses and QueryClient isolation.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but the skill should be labeled for network examples and script content.
